root_cause.text: The IPv6 Neighbor Discovery implementation unconditionally accepted and applied the hop limit from received Router Advertisement (RA) messages, allowing a remote attacker to maliciously reduce the interface's hop limit to a low value and cause legitimate packets to be dropped.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `net/ipv6/ndisc.c` modifies `ndisc_router_discovery` by adding a condition `if (in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit < ra_msg->icmph.icmp6_hop_limit)` before setting `in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit`, ensuring the hop limit can only be increased, not decreased.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The IPv6 Neighbor Discovery implementation unconditionally accepted and applied the hop limit from received Router Advertisement (RA) messages, allowing a remote attacker to maliciously reduce the interface's hop limit to a low value and cause legitimate packets to be dropped.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `net/ipv6/ndisc.c` modifies `ndisc_router_discovery` by adding a condition `if (in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit < ra_msg->icmph.icmp6_hop_limit)` before setting `in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit`, ensuring the hop limit can only be increased, not decreased.

intent_record.intent_summary: Prevent Router Advertisements from maliciously or erroneously lowering the IPv6 hop limit.
intent_record.what: Added a condition to only update the interface hop limit if the new value from the Router Advertisement is higher than the current value, logging a warning otherwise.
intent_record.how: Prevents the IPv6 hop limit from being lowered by Router Advertisements, protecting against potential network misconfigurations or attacks.

grouping_result.intent: Prevent Router Advertisements from maliciously or erroneously lowering the IPv6 hop limit.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by preventing Router Advertisements from improperly lowering the IPv6 hop limit, which is exactly the issue described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: Unconditionally applies the hop limit from the RA message to the interface configuration, allowing it to be reduced maliciously.
Unconditionally sets the hop limit in the routing metrics based on the RA message.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement unconditionally applies the hop limit from the RA message to the interface configuration, which allows it to be reduced maliciously by a remote attacker.
This statement unconditionally sets the hop limit in the routing metrics based on the RA message.

bic_trace_step.reason: The function `ndisc_router_discovery` unconditionally updates `in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit` and route metrics using `ra_msg->icmph.icmp6_hop_limit` if it is non-zero, lacking any safety bounds or checks to prevent it from being maliciously set to a very low value.

net: Abstract away all dst_entry metrics accesses.

Use helper functions to hide all direct accesses, especially writes,
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit defb3519a64141608725e2dac5a5aa9a3c644bae
Author: David S. Miller <davem@davemloft.net>
Date:   Wed Dec 8 21:16:57 2010 -0800

    net: Abstract away all dst_entry metrics accesses.
    
    Use helper functions to hide all direct accesses, especially writes,
    to dst_entry metrics values.
    
    This will allow us to:
    
    1) More easily change how the metrics are stored.
    
    2) Implement COW for metrics.
    
    In particular this will help us put metrics into the inetpeer
bic_trace_step.reason: The code unconditionally sets 'in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit' and the hop limit metric to 'ra_msg->icmph.icmp6_hop_limit' as long as it is non-zero. It lacks checks to ensure the value does not decrease below a safe threshold, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

Author: Changli Gao <xiaosuo@gmail.com>

    net-next: remove useless union keyword
    
    remove useless union keyword in rtable, rt6_info and dn_route.

    Signed-off-by: Changli Gao <xiaosuo@gmail.com>

bic_trace_step.reason: The function ndisc_router_discovery() unconditionally assigns the hop limit from the RA message (in6_dev->cnf.hop_limit = ra_msg->icmph.icmp6_hop_limit) without checking if the new value is suspiciously low or if it violates a user-configured minimum limit. The commit only removes the 'u' union wrapper from the 'dst' member.
